evals.name: react-testing-conventions
evals.prompt: Write a rule file for testing conventions
evals.expected_output: A .claude/rules/testing.md file with paths: frontmatter scoped to test files, documenting the Arrange/Act/Assert comment pattern, factory functions, stub-based test doubles (no mocking frameworks), Vitest globals, and @file references to helpers/factories.ts and helpers/stubs.tsx

evals.name: go-api-endpoint-patterns
evals.prompt: Write a rule file for API endpoint patterns
evals.expected_output: A .claude/rules/ file documenting handler struct pattern, constructor injection, response envelope usage, validation flow, error mapping with errors.Is, middleware chain, and @file references to exemplar handlers

evals.name: go-error-handling-with-overlap
evals.prompt: Write a rule file for error handling and domain error patterns
evals.expected_output: A .claude/rules/ file that detects overlap with existing api.md and logging.md rules, scopes itself to domain/service error patterns (apperror package, error constructors, mapping in handlers), and includes complementary references to existing rules

evals.name: rust-user-provided-conventions
evals.prompt: Write a rule file for error handling. We use thiserror for library errors and anyhow for app errors. All public functions return Result<T, Error>. Never use unwrap() in production code, only in tests.
evals.expected_output: A .claude/rules/ file that incorporates the user-stated conventions (thiserror, anyhow, no unwrap in prod) AND discovers additional patterns from the codebase (domain error enums, IntoResponse impls, #[source] usage), with !IMPORTANT markers on the unwrap constraint

evals.name: spring-boot-vague-request
evals.prompt: Write a rule file for this project
evals.expected_output: The agent should push back and ask for a specific topic rather than trying to document everything. If it does write something, it should be narrowly scoped to the most non-obvious conventions rather than a kitchen-sink document.
